mechanism_narrative: Positive recent growth in retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when measured from the latest available RSAFS release.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of out-of-sample observations or the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above a restrictive threshold, discount-rate pressure dominates earnings support and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns exceed unconditional mean returns by more than 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate while policy rates remain high indicates late-cycle labor deterioration without immediate monetary relief, reducing expected earnings and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value is 4.1 and latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value is 4.33
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not produce lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than either UNRATE increases alone or high FEDFUNDS alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when INDPRO momentum is positive.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has a non-positive out-of-sample coefficient for 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below a contraction threshold signal weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction-linked earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1400 triggered observations have median 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than the full-sample median or a hit rate below 55% for negative-return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating consumer prices combined with continued payroll gains identifies a soft-landing mix of lower discount-rate risk and intact income growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month CPIAUCSL inflation decelerates while PAYEMS increases by more than 100 thousand over 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot value is 319.775 and latest PAYEMS snapshot value is 159218
failure_condition: Reject if the joint disinflation-plus-payroll trigger has lower average 20-day returns than unconditional returns or if its positive-return hit rate is below 55% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below a low-stress threshold signals limited near-term recession risk and supports short-horizon risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-below-4.2 triggered observations fail to produce a positive 5-day mean return or if the signal hit rate is below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP levels combined with restrictive policy rates indicate demand resilience that delays rate cuts and raises the probability of valuation pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 is above its 4-quarter moving average while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 snapshot value is 23536.293 and latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value is 4.33
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-GDPC1 and high-FEDFUNDS condition is followed by higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than high-GDPC1 alone in walk-forward evaluation.
